runs.label: perf-qwq-32b-iq2m-run1
runs.output:
Keep your answer in English.

RISC and CISC architectures represent two distinct philosophies in processor design, each with its own advantages and trade-offs. RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) architectures, exemplified by MIPS, SPARC, and ARM, emphasize simplicity and efficiency. They use a small set of carefully chosen instructions optimized for fast execution, typically with uniform instruction lengths and straightforward pipelines. This philosophy allows for deeper pipelining and higher clock speeds, as well as easier compiler optimization.

runs.label: perf-qwq-32b-iq2m-run2
runs.output:
Keep your answer in English and avoid markdown formatting.

The RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) and CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) architectures represent contrasting design philosophies rooted in different eras of computing. RISC architectures, exemplified by MIPS, SPARC, and early ARM designs, prioritize simplicity and efficiency. Their instruction sets are streamlined, with a focus on orthogonal, general-purpose instructions that can be executed quickly. This philosophy assumes that complex tasks can be broken down into simpler instructions,
